title: Credit Risk Manager (Retail, Credit Cards)
description:
Responsibilities:

  • Own and continuously enhance retail credit risk frameworks for credit card underwriting and portfolio management, ensuring alignment with regulatory standards and internal risk appetite.
  • Lead the drafting, review, and implementation of credit card–specific credit policies, underwriting criteria, and risk controls; assess policy effectiveness and recommend enhancements.
  • Partner closely with risk modelling and analytics teams to drive the development, validation, implementation, and ongoing monitoring of credit risk models (e.g. scorecards, Basel, ECL), including review of model outputs and assumptions.
  • Conduct in-depth credit card portfolio analytics, covering approval trends, risk grade migration, delinquency and loss performance, vintage analysis, early warning indicators, and concentration risks.
  • Manage risk appetite and limit frameworks for credit card portfolios, including limit calibration, stress testing analysis, breach monitoring, and escalation.
  • Provide risk input and challenge to business, underwriting, and product teams on credit card strategy reviews, policy changes, new product initiatives, and portfolio optimisation proposals.
  • Produce and review recurring and ad-hoc risk reports for senior management, synthesising complex portfolio data into clear risk insights and actionable recommendations.
  • Prepare and present materials for governance forums, committees, and internal stakeholders, highlighting portfolio performance, underwriting outcomes, key risk metrics, and emerging risk themes.
  • Support junior team members through guidance and review of analytical work, contributing to team capability building and knowledge sharing.
  • Drive process improvements and automation initiatives to enhance efficiency, data quality, and scalability of credit risk monitoring and reporting.

Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s degree or above in a quantitative discipline (e.g. Statistics, Mathematics, Financial Engineering, Quantitative Finance, Data Analytics) from a reputable institution.
  • Minimum 3 years of relevant working full-time experience in retail credit products, such as credit cards and unsecured personal loans, or other financial risk domains.
  • Proficiency in data and analytical tools such as SQL is a plus.
  • Experience with additional analytics or BI tools is an advantage.
  • Familiarity with credit card–relevant credit risk concepts, including scorecards, Basel, ECL, and stress testing.
  • Demonstrated ability to independently conduct complex analyses and translate results into clear risk recommendations for decision-makers.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to provide constructive risk challenge to business and product partners.
  • Detail-oriented, well-organised, and able to operate effectively in a fast-paced, structured environment.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

Note:
Kindly note that you can only be considered for one role at a time with any of the companies within our group. If you have applied for other jobs with the group, you will be considered for roles in the order of your application

postedCompany.description: Maribank Singapore Pte Ltd is a full digital bank, regulated under MAS, providing financial services to retail and corporate customers.
postedCompany.name: MARIBANK SINGAPORE PRIVATE LIMITED
